name: model
summary: Plain typed data class for an entity, with identity — the value object passed around the system.
description: Creates a data model class with identity support
problem: You need a typed in-memory representation of an entity to flow through your application — something the data layer hydrates, that controllers serialize, and that listeners can introspect.
appliesWhen: An entity needs a typed shape used across the system
You want consumers to treat it as immutable data, not a database row
You need a stable place to hang typed properties for the entity
You want compiler/IDE-friendly access (no associative arrays floating around)
avoidWhen: You don't need a typed shape — the data is consumed once and discarded
The shape is highly dynamic per record (use an array or object with __get)
You're storing config or settings (use a config object, not a model)
examples: I need a typed Order class
Create a value object for subscriptions
Define the shape of a Payout
tradeoffs: One small file. Without persistence wiring it's just a data class — pair with phpnomad/datastore (or phpnomad/database-datastore for the full stack) when you need storage.
outputs: A {name} model class with identity helpers
Stub typed property declarations to fill in
postApply: Add typed properties for each field on the entity
Pair with phpnomad/model-adapter and phpnomad/database-handler if backing this with a database

name.aiHint: Singular PascalCase. No 'Model' suffix — naming is canonical (Order, not OrderModel).
